title: create volume mount hostdir if it does not exist
body:
In the case where the host dir of a volume mount does not exist, we
should create it.  We use 0755 permissions on the dir creation.  This
mimics docker behavior.

Resolves: #1324

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: I don't like this.  I believe this behaviour is wrong, and allows users to have a typo and get unexpected behaviour.  I think we should vary from docker and report this as an error, if the source dir/file does not exist.
